content.text:
<user_query>
You are a reviewer for a robotics RL config tuning task. Objective: explain current selected hyperparameters in `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py` and propose sweep ranges/intervals for stability under push disturbances and command switches to zero velocity. Please check for: objective completeness, consistency with parent config inheritance, hidden assumptions, and risks from changing specific knobs (e.g., probability simplex constraints, task vs AMP blend, disabled stability rewards). Suggest if additional source files are needed to avoid misleading values.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

Git repo: /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl

?? .DS_Store

 M humanoid-gym/humanoid/utils/video_processor.py

</git_status>

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

Findings (ordered by severity):

1. `commands.new_sample_methods` in the stability config is effectively shadowed.
- In `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py:73-89`, this variant sets `straight_prob=0.09`, `backward_prob=0.04`, `stand_prob=0.80`, `turn_prob=0.07`.
- That setting is not what the sampler uses for this task registration, because `R01V12SAAMPWith4DoFArmsAndHeadFullScenesCfg` inherits `amp_scene_tag_ratio=[0.1,0.4,0.2,0.3]` in `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes.py:73` and `R01V12AMPEnv` is the active environment in `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/__init__.py:213-216`.
- `R01V12AMPEnv._resample_commands` selects the scene-tag branch whenever `amp_scene_tag_ratio` exists, so the `new_sample_methods` weights are bypassed (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py:165-167` and `173-177`).
- Impact: the effective stand probability is ~10%, not 80%; this is a major objective drift for “zero-velocity stabilization.”

2. Explicit zero-velocity switching knobs from base AMP config are not active here.
- Base AMP resampling does `zero_linvel_prob`/`stop_command_prob` masking (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py:966-979`), but `R01V12AMPEnv` overrides and exits that path when scene tags are configured (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py:165-167`).
- `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_config.py:263-265` sets those knobs in the parent, but they are not applied for this stability variant due the override chain.
- Impact: current setup does not realize “command switches to zero velocity” via these params; only `stand` tag selection can create zero command samples.

3. Several stability-focused reward terms are fully disabled despite being documented as tunable.
- `legged_robot._prepare_reward_function` removes zero scales and only registers active terms (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py:930-940`).
- In the stability config, scales for `stand_still`, `orientation`, `ang_vel_xy`, `lin_vel_z`, and `base_height` are `0.0` (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py:117,122,132,142,147`), so those terms are not computed at all; `torso_orientation` and `torso_ang_vel_xy_penalty` are inherited/overridden from parent, but `default_joint_pos` also `0.0` here (`...:127` and `152`).
- Impact: you may think you are sweeping stability coefficients, but many are dead knobs in this variant until set non-zero.

4. AMP/style guidance is fully disabled for this variant.
- `amp_task_reward_lerp=1.0` in this variant (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py:163`) with no inherited penalty contribution.
- `AMPDiscriminator` computes final reward as `(1-lerp)*disc_r + lerp*task_r`; with `lerp=1.0`, the discriminator term contributes zero (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/algo/amp_ppo/amp_discriminator.py:94-97`).
- Impact: this is consistent with task-priority behavior, but it is a strong architectural assumption for stability tuning; no AMP style regularization remains.

5. Command-tag ratio math has no validation guardrails.
- Scene-tag branch accumulates intervals from zip’d lists, then samples by range thresholds without checking sum-to-1 or full coverage (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py:173-189`).
- Impact: edits to `amp_scene_tag_ratio` can silently misallocate probability mass (gaps/overflows), so sweep experiments can produce invalid distributions unexpectedly.

6. `leg_only_control=True` and randomized unactuated joints likely increase disturbance recovery difficulty.
- `leg_only_control` is set true in stability config (`...stability_priority.py:49`), and torso/arms are still zero-torque (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py:453-455`).
- The same variant enables non-controlled random DOF reset (`...:68`) and `_randomize_non_controlled_joint_pos` can perturb them at reset over joint limits with margin (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_amp_env.py:601-628`).
- Impact: recovery tasks under push may be tougher than expected and may require stronger uprightness/torque/reactivity rewards or reduced joint randomization.

7. Push sampling uses integer-exclusive upper bound semantics and dt scaling.
- New push intervals are sampled from `torch.randint(low, high)` and cast by `1/dt` (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/base/legged_robot.py:557-563`), plus `low/high` from config are in seconds but used as int bounds (`863-863`+).
- Impact: current `[5.0,10.0]` yields 5–9 seconds (not including 10 s) and effective timing is discretized by `dt`; treat interval bounds as design-sensitive in sweeps.

Objective completeness & inheritance consistency check:

- Inheritance is clear enough to explain why the effective behavior differs from local intuition.
- The most actionable inconsistency is that intent is currently expressed in `commands.new_sample_methods` while actual sampling is driven by `env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` for this environment class.
- There is no evidence of a support-polygon reward term in the env codebase (`rg "support_polygon"` returns no matches), so any polygon-based objective is not yet implemented and should be treated as a future work item.

Recommended immediate interpretation for current run behavior (what is effectively active now):

- Push knobs: active (`max_push_vel_xy`, `max_push_ang_vel`, `push_interval_t_range` at `humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_sa_amp_config_with_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority.py:55,60,65`).
- Command mix: effectively from `amp_scene_tag_ratio` in parent (`...full_scenes.py:73`) as scene tags, not from `commands.new_sample_methods` in the stability file.
- Reward shaping that matters: currently mostly tracking plus any non-zero inherited terms; the explicitly listed “stability” scales set to zero are inactive unless changed.
- AMP/task blend: pure task reward (`amp_task_reward_lerp=1.0`).

Suggested first runnable sweep plan (for stability under push + zero-velocity command transitions), with minimal-risk boundaries:

- For push disturbance robustness:
  - `domain_rand.max_push_vel_xy`: `0.5–1.0`
  - `domain_rand.max_push_ang_vel`: `0.0–0.5`
  - `domain_rand.push_interval_t_range`: keep narrower/higher-frequency for robustness (`3.0–8.0`) or wider for rare large impulses (`5.0–12.0`)
- For command exposure (if keeping scene-tag routing):
  - `env.amp_scene_tag_ratio` override directly to match intent, e.g. test stand-heavy variants in `[0.2,0.6]` for stand component and renormalize to 1.0.
- For stability terms (activate currently disabled ones):
  - `rewards.scales.stand_still`: `[-3.0,-1.0]`
  - `rewards.scales.orientation`: `[-5.0,-2.0]`
  - `rewards.scales.torso_orientation`: `[-5.0,-1.0]`
  - `rewards.scales.ang_vel_xy`: `[-2.0,-0.5]`
  - `rewards.scales.torso_ang_vel_xy_penalty`: `[0.001,0.01]`
  - `rewards.scales.lin_vel_z`: `[-2.0,-0.5]`
  - `rewards.scales.base_height`: `[-10.0,-2.0]`
  - `rewards.scales.default_joint_pos`: `[0.2,1.0]`
- Keep a small tracking budget during early stability sweeps:
  - `rewards.scales.tracking_avg_lin_vel`: keep around `1.0–1.8`

Open questions / assumptions before proceeding:

- Confirm whether you want to keep scene-tag sampling (`amp_scene_tag_ratio`) as the source of command distribution, or switch back to `commands.new_sample_methods` semantics.
- Confirm whether command-zero transition behavior should be “scene tag only” or include explicit `zero_linvel_prob/stop_command_prob` semantics.
- Confirm whether pure task reward (`amp_task_reward_lerp=1.0`) is intentional, or you want limited AMP pressure retained.
- Confirm if support-polygon reward should be added now; there is currently no support-polygon reward implementation in the codebase, so it would require new env reward logic (`humanoid-gym/humanoid/envs/r01_amp/r01_v12_amp_env.py` or sibling env) plus config scale plumbing.

If you want, next I can prepare a minimal, low-risk patch plan (config-only first) and a separate robust patch (with `r01_v12_amp_env.py` precedence/validation changes) so the effective knobs and the stated intent align exactly.
